MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JULIE FARRUGIA, Esq. of Stoll, Nussbaum & Polakov (Tel: 310-996-7500) for plaintiffs;
Atty. ALICE SMITH, Esq. of Yoka Smith LLP (Tel: 213-427-2300) for defendant The Pep Boys Manny Moe & Jack of California LLC;
Atty. DMITRIY KOPELVICH, Esq. of Dykema Gossett LLP (Tel: 213-457-1800) for defendant Estla, Inc.
Atty. MICHELLE CHEA, Esq. of Yukevich Cavanaugh (Tel: 213-362-7777) for defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT THE PEP BOYS MANNY MOE & JACK OF CALIFORNIA LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Pep Boys' demurrers to the first amended complaint (FAC) are overruled. As already discussed in overruling similar demurrers by the Uber defendants earlier this month, the FAC adequately pleads negligence and strict liability by defendants. The "specificity" the Pep Boys demand (Memo. 7:8-9) is required in California's courts only for fraud and a very limited set of other claims.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Argued. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

The Uber defendants' demurrers to the first amended complaint (FAC) are overruled. The FAC pleads that the Uber defendants "marketed and rented the subject vehicle to Plaintiffs." (Id. at p. 6 [negligence], p. 9 [strict products liability].) The Uber defendants argue from a Form 10-K that they do not rent or distributed Tesla vehicles (Memo. 6:8), but the Form 10-K is not exclusive and plaintiffs' allegations must be accepted as true for purposes of ruling on a demurrer. (Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Corp. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604.)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

The Uber Defendants' demurrer is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41.
